title: rouya polygama
intro: Rouya polygama, commonly known as  the African tea plant, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of East Africa. The health benefits of Rouya polygama include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Rouya polygama is traditionally used for its therapeutic actions, including treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Rouya polygama include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Rouya polygama can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like hives, itching, and swelling. In severe cases, excessive consumption can lead to cardiovascular problems, including increased heart rate and blood pressure. Long-term misuse may also cause kidney damage and liver toxicity, ultimately affecting overall organ health. Additionally, improper use of the plant can interact with other medications, reducing their effectiveness or increasing the risk of adverse interactions.
